article_title: Cisco Patches Sixth SD-WAN Zero-Day of 2026 as CISA Adds CVE-2026-20182 to KEV With Three-Day Federal Deadline

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Exemplary cybersecurity submission with textbook Rule 9 discipline. CISA primary alert + NVD detail page + Rapid7 technical analysis all cited as primary publications. Every specific verified verbatim: CVE-2026-20182, CVSS 10.0, vector AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H, CWE-287, May 14 disclosure, May 17 federal remediation deadline, full fixed-version list (20.9.9.1 / 20.12.5.4 / 20.12.6.2 / 20.12.7.1 / 20.15.4.4 / 20.15.5.2 / 20.18.2.2 / 26.1.1.1), 'vdaemon' service + UDP port 12346 + DTLS, vHub device type with 'no verification code' verbatim, 'not a patch bypass of CVE-2026-20127' + 'similar part of the vdaemon networking stack' verbatim, 'become an authenticated peer' verbatim, NETCONF post-exploit path, UAT-8616 attribution + 'highly sophisticated' + 'limited attacks' + 'add SSH keys, modify NETCONF configurations, and escalate to root privileges' verbatim, Operational Relay Box (ORB) framing, full sibling-CVE list (20127, 20128, 20122, 20133, 2022-20775), 'sixth SD-WAN flaw' framing, 'three days' KEV deadline. Internal cross-reference to 2026-02/28-cisco-sd-wan-zero-day-exploited-for-three-years-before-patch... verified to exist.

source-0.status: CISA primary KEV alert (May 14). Verifies CVE-2026-20182 entry, 'Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Authentication Bypass Vulnerability'.

source-1.status: NVD primary entry. Verifies CVSS 10.0 base score, full vector string, Cisco as CNA.

source-2.status: Rapid7 technical analysis. Verifies: 'not a patch bypass / similar part of the vdaemon networking stack' verbatim, vHub device-type 'no verification code' analysis, 'become an authenticated peer' verbatim, NETCONF login path, full fixed-version table, CWE-287 classification.

source-3.status: The Hacker News. Verifies: CISA description quote verbatim, May 17 FCEB deadline, 'add SSH keys, modify NETCONF configurations, and escalate to root privileges' Talos quote verbatim, ORB infrastructure overlap framing.

source-4.status: SecurityWeek. Verifies: 'sixth' framing, UAT-8616 'highly sophisticated' + 'limited attacks' verbatim, full sibling-CVE list (20127, 20128, 20122, 20133, 2022-20775), 'three days' KEV deadline.

source-5.status: Help Net Security. Verifies: port 12346 + UDP + DTLS framing for the vdaemon service, UAT-8616 attribution tying both CVEs together.

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent structure (Overview / What We Know / Active Exploitation and Attribution / CISA's Three-Day Deadline / What We Don't Know). Technical, precise, neutral. Length appropriate for News (725 words). All inline citations point to the source that actually contains the claim — no compound-citation issues found.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of CVE-2026-20182. Correctly cross-references the February 28 article on CVE-2026-20127 as the related earlier disclosure.

content_preview.title: Cisco Patches Sixth SD-WAN Zero-Day of 2026 as CISA Adds CVE-2026-20182 to KEV With Three-Day Federal Deadline
content_preview.summary: A second authentication bypass in the same vdaemon stack as February's CVE-2026-20127 carries a CVSS 10.0 and is being exploited by the same UAT-8616 cluster, Cisco and Talos disclosed on May 14.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Cisco on May 14 disclosed a second critical authentication bypass in its Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager products, tracked as CVE-2026-20182 and carrying the maximum CVSS v3.1 base score of 10.0, according to the [NIST National Vulnerability Database](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-20182). The same day, the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency added the flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog and gave Federal Civilian Executive Branch a...
